references.cue: Calmer, more consistent interface with dimmer navigation chrome and easier scanning.
references.adapted: Kept the queue visible but dimmed secondary chrome so the active task plane carries the hierarchy.
references.rejected: Did not mirror issue-triage side-by-side density because the workspace already has a dedicated queue lane.

references.cue: Sidebar simplification, consistent tabs, and full-focus posture for the main work area.
references.adapted: Used one dominant task plane and kept the dock stable instead of splitting action across multiple panels.
references.rejected: Did not add extra top-level navigation inside the task shell because the shell already has a route-family rail.

references.cue: Saved preferences, search, and structured dashboard overview.
references.adapted: Preserved selected anchor and shell continuity rather than re-deciding local state on every route change.
references.rejected: Did not introduce grid/list toggles inside the task shell because the task plane needs one editorial reading path.

references.cue: Dense but readable structured data with disciplined toolbar and row hierarchy.
references.adapted: Used compact row and stack grammar with explicit labels before details.
references.rejected: Did not render the task plane as a table because consequence review and delta context require sectional reading.

references.cue: Clear headings, explicit focus styles, visible labels, and language that makes sense out of context.
references.adapted: Kept stack headings literal, preserved focus-visible styling, and avoided shorthand-only status writing.
references.rejected: Did not rely on color or placement alone to signal delta, blocked, or superseded state.
